talk_metadata.talk_title: Elucidating Broad Cell-Cell Interactions via 3D Super Cell Graphs for Prostate Cancer Risk Stratification
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't have an independent cohort, this is a limitation in this study"
Speaker said: "We used leave-one-out cross-validation; we haven't considered independent validation"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential loss of detailed cell‑level interaction information when aggregating into super‑cells

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of independent validation cohort for the 3D super‑cell graph signature suggests an opportunity for CrisPRO to provide robust cross‑cohort validation using its computational spine.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker admitted: "We don't have independent cohort"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑center data and assess model generalizability across cohorts.

unresolved_questions: How does the super‑cell aggregation affect specific cell‑type interaction signals?
Can the model be extended to biopsy specimens with limited tissue?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI-Driven Spatial Analysis of 1,100 Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma Patients' H&E Slides Uncovers Three Tumor Subtypes with Markedly Distinct Tumor Microenvironments and Survival Patterns
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The correlation is in the lower range"
Speaker said: "We have moderate correlation for many genes, especially in external validation"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited predictive power for low‑expressed genes due to low variance across spots

key_data_gaps: No direct validation of predicted spatial clusters with orthogonal proteomic stains

crispro_opportunity.description: Model struggles with low‑variance genes; CrisPRO could prioritize high‑impact gene sets and improve prediction of clinically actionable pathways.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted moderate correlations and difficulty predicting low‑expressed genes
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate pathway‑level priors to boost signal for weakly expressed targets.

crispro_opportunity.description: Spatial clusters predict immunotherapy response; CrisPRO could combine with existing PD‑L1 IHC data to refine patient selection.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed AUC 0.74 for treatment response prediction using spatial clusters
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse multi‑modal biomarkers (spatial transcriptomics + IHC) for a composite predictor.

unresolved_questions: How will the model perform on other head and neck subsites not represented in the training set?
Can the spatial transcriptomics predictions be calibrated against protein‑level measurements?

assets_to_track: Head and neck AI biomarker pipelines

talk_metadata.talk_title: Pan-Cancer Prediction of Response to Immune Checkpoint Blockade from Histopathology
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't have the ICB measure for the dataset we have"
Speaker said: "We are happy to collaborate if you have the data"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: TAH‑HOT signature derived only from melanoma
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential lack of tumor‑type specific immune contexture beyond melanoma

key_data_gaps: No direct ICB response measurements in the primary cohort

crispro_opportunity.description: TAH‑HOT relies on composite hotness score; CrisPRO could provide a more granular, pathway‑level hotness metric derived from histology.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted the signature was derived only from melanoma but generalized well
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model pan‑cancer immune signatures with explicit pathway annotations.

unresolved_questions: How does the model perform on metastatic lesions versus primary tumors?
What is the minimal number of tiles needed for robust hotness prediction?

talk_metadata.talk_title: A Large Scale Multi-Target Deep Learning Model for Virtual Genomic Profiling in Colorectal Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We still see confounders with different scanners and sites"
Speaker said: "Biopsy versus resection slides show differential performance"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Virtual genomics prediction for low‑prevalence mutations (e.g., APC)
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Morphological phenotype for ubiquitous early driver mutations is weak, limiting discriminative power

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Prediction of 463 gene mutations from H&E
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mixed biopsy and resection specimens without stratification
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering for rare mutation sub‑cohorts

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Multi‑task transformer model for virtual genomics
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on frozen foundation models; performance converges regardless of initial backbone at large scale, reducing unique IP advantage
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad pan‑cancer mutation screening may dilute regulatory focus on high‑impact biomarkers

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high AUC for MSI (0.98) yet admitted that biopsy slides reduce performance, suggesting overreliance on resection morphology

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation of virtual genomics predictions

crispro_opportunity.description: Low AUC for common mutations like APC indicates a salvage opportunity; CrisPRO could integrate pathway‑level features to boost prediction for early drivers.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted APC had low AUC due to lack of morphological distinction
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model hierarchical pathway signatures to infer early driver status from subtle histologic cues.

crispro_opportunity.description: Biopsy versus resection performance gap suggests redesigning future virtual genomics trials to stratify by specimen type.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted differential performance between biopsy and resection slides
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide calibrated risk scores per specimen type, informing trial inclusion criteria.

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal number of positive mutation examples needed for stable performance across genes?
Can the model predict variant allele frequency thresholds?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Georgia Institute of Technology / Emory University
talk_metadata.talk_title: Peritumoral Collagen Microarchitecture is Differentially Prognostic of Biochemical Recurrence Across Populations in Prostate Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We cannot use one size fit all model for these types of data set"
Speaker said: "The African‑American features were able to stratify risk in the whole cohort, but European‑American features were not"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of robust texture features that capture prognostic variation in European‑American cohort

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Peritumoral collagen microarchitecture as prognostic biomarker
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Equal number of AA and EA patients but no stratification by Gleason score or tumor location
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standardized collagen staining (e.g., Picrosirius Red) for ground truth validation
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small overall sample (202 patients) limits power for subgroup analyses

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Model trained on H&E only; validation against only unpaired Mason Trichrome shows ~75‑80% accuracy, indicating limited robustness
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Relies on manual tumor annotations; scalability to routine pathology uncertain

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that African‑American collagen features predict whole‑cohort outcomes yet admitted lack of validation for European‑American features, highlighting contradictory model generalizability

key_data_gaps: No orthogonal collagen stain (e.g., Picrosirius Red) for ground truth

crispro_opportunity.description: European‑American collagen signature underperforms; CrisPRO could integrate multi‑ethnic training data and pathway‑level features to improve cross‑population robustness.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted only one shared feature between AA and EA signatures
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's multi‑task framework can learn shared and population‑specific representations simultaneously.

crispro_opportunity.description: Current collagen segmentation lacks validation; CrisPRO can incorporate multimodal stains (Mason Trichrome, Picrosirius Red) to strengthen model confidence.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned pathologist validation at ~75‑80% accuracy
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse H&E with auxiliary stain embeddings for more accurate collagen detection.

unresolved_questions: Would incorporating Gleason score improve the collagen‑based model?
How does tumor location within the prostate affect peritumoral collagen patterns?

talk_metadata.talk_title: PathIO, a deep learning pathomics framework for personalized immunotherapy selection and outcome prediction in metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have not fine‑tuned the foundation models; we only extracted features"
Speaker said: "We still need prospective validation in clinical settings"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Prognostic risk prediction for ICB‑treated metastatic NSCLC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mixed first‑line monotherapy and combination therapy patients without stratified randomization
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standard PD‑L1 IHC scores for direct comparison
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential overfitting due to limited number of events per cohort

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Tissue habitat map + random survival forest pipeline
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on manually annotated tissue regions; similar pipelines exist in other academic groups
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Model currently prognostic only; predictive utility for therapy selection remains exploratory

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented strong OS/PFS stratification yet acknowledged that the model is purely prognostic and not yet validated for predictive treatment selection

key_data_gaps: No prospective trial demonstrating predictive benefit for monotherapy vs combination
Limited external validation beyond three cohorts

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate PD‑L1 IHC and other established biomarkers into PathIO risk model to create a predictive, not just prognostic, tool.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted need for prospective validation and comparison with existing biomarkers
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multimodal data (pathology, radiology, IHC) within a unified predictive framework.

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO's interpretability modules to provide clearer attribution of risk to specific tissue habitats, addressing clinicians' demand for explainability.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted interpretability as a current limitation compared to foundation models
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO offers attention‑map visualizations linked to pathway activity scores.

unresolved_questions: How does PathIO perform on biopsy‑only specimens?
Can the model be adapted to predict response to emerging combination regimens?
